title: HelloNet campaign — new malicious modules launched through the ViPNet update system
summary: We identified targeted infection attempts against large Russian organizations using the ViPNet update system (a software suite for creating secure networks).

title: GoSerpent: a persistent threat evolves with sophisticated data collection and exfiltration
summary: Two-phase attacks with the GoSerpent backdoor, Stowaway RAT, ThumbcacheService and other tools aim to steal data from government entities in Southeast Asia.

title: OkoBot: new sophisticated malware framework targets cryptocurrency users
summary: Kaspersky GReAT experts dissect the new OkoBot campaign targeting cryptocurrency users. This complex framework employs TookPS, exfiltrates seed phrases, monitors Chromium-based browsers, and installs various malware strains, including the Rilide stealer.

title: Threat landscape for industrial automation systems. Q1 2026
summary: This report contains industrial threat statistics for Q1 2026, including industrial threat distribution by type, source, region and industry.

title: When checking the URL isn’t enough: a Device Code Phishing attack via a Microsoft website
summary: The OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant specification was designed to streamline authentication for Smart TVs, IoT devices, and printers. Today, threat actors are weaponizing it.

title: Armored Likho digging a snake pit: inside the covert BusySnake Stealer campaign
summary: An inside look at the active Armored Likho APT campaign. The attackers are using spear-phishing, AI-generated loaders, and a new Python-based tool, BusySnake Stealer, to target organizations in Russia, Kazakhstan, and Brazil.

title: Missed incidents, persistent threats, and response gaps: Insights from compromise assessment projects
summary: Kaspersky Compromise Assessment specialists analyze trends from the service's 2025 projects and provide tips on how to enhance your organization's security.

title: The SOC Files: ScreenConnect masked as freeware. An inside look at a large-scale campaign
summary: Kaspersky experts have uncovered a malicious network infrastructure for delivering AsyncRAT. The Trojan is dropped via compromised ScreenConnect software. In this post, we break down the infection chain and analyze the C2 infrastructure.

title: OpenClaw: risks for the users and how to mitigate them
summary: Researching OpenClaw vulnerabilities, malicious skills, and other security issues with the popular agent, and providing tips on how to mitigate them.

title: ToddyCat: your hidden email assistant. Part 2
summary: An in-depth analysis of Umbrij, a new tool used by the ToddyCat APT group to compromise corporate email communications in Gmail. The attack targeted OAuth authorization tokens, allowing threat actors to gain access to Google services.
